headline: Agent infrastructure and the Model Context Protocol dominate the week as system architecture breaks out alongside a wave of tool-standardization work.
body: System architecture leads the rotation and registers as a breakout, paired with infrastructure techniques RLHF and KV cache quantization that also break out this window. The clearest theme is agent plumbing: Google's WebMCP proposes a web standard letting agents call typed actions instead of automating the DOM [10], MCP tutorials and ADK-plus-Looker agent configuration work fill in the client-server stack [4][11], and Cursor describes running 128 coding agents in parallel with inter-agent review [7]. Local execution is a parallel current, with Ollama leading among tools and a masterclass on running Gemma 4 agents privately via Docker and a frontier-to-local fallback strategy [9]. New entrants text-to-CAD, faithful generation, and cross-modal prediction signal fresh technique-side activity, while Claude's Fable 5 draws backlash over opaque guardrails and model fallbacks [1].
